title: Crispy Smoky Cheese Kenyan Stir-fry
description: A quick-fix, crispy-smoked dish featuring a blend of low-sodium-friendly, liquid cheese with smoky flavors, ideal for a savory side or light meal.

ingredients.name: Kikuyu (or any other leafy green vegetable)

instructions: Wash and chop the chicken breast into bite-sized pieces. Chop onion and garlic.
Heat oil in a pressure cooker over medium heat. Add onions, cook until translucent.
Add garlic and cook for another minute. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Push vegetables aside on one side of the pot and mix cheese with paprika in a bowl.
Pour liquid cheese mixture into the pot and stir gently without breaking up the pieces.
Gently fold in chicken, ensuring that it's well-coated with the sauce. Cover and cook under pressure for 20 minutes or until chicken is fully cooked.
Once cooked, open the lid of the cooker slowly to prevent steam escaping. Add chopped vegetables back into the pot gently to ensure they stay crispy.
Cook for an additional 1-2 minutes until vegetables are slightly wilted and the sauce is thickened.
Serve hot with steamed rice or as a side dish.
